overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted academic and professional presence across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon, totaling 3,580 followers. His content is deeply specialized around computational social science, disinformation research, and academic career opportunities. While LinkedIn provides the highest absolute reach and interaction counts due to a larger follower base, Bluesky registers the highest relative audience engagement rate, showing a highly active and receptive network.

contentThemes.description: Research findings, methodological approaches, and tools (like CooRnet v2.0) designed to detect coordinated link-sharing campaigns.
Announcements regarding research grants, postdoctoral hiring, and PhD research opportunities.
Sharing upcoming presentations at specialized academic conferences and fostering community discussions.

recommendations: Cross-promote major software releases like CooRnet v2.0 on LinkedIn to leverage its larger, professional network.
Increase posting frequency on Mastodon to build stronger momentum with its niche, technical academic community.
Utilize rich media, such as direct links to PDFs or visual infographics of research papers, to further boost click-through rates.

platformInsights.bluesky: The most engaging platform with an average engagement rate of 6.14%. It has a highly active community that frequently reposts research findings and academic opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Serves a smaller, highly technical audience. While overall volume is lower, technical announcements like software updates perform exceptionally well (5.64% engagement rate).
platformInsights.linkedin: The main hub for reach, boasting 1,850 followers. It yields powerful raw numbers for institutional milestones, such as securing grants or advertising jobs.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
